name: Chocolate & Peanut Butter Crescents
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, cocoa powder, and salt in a bowl.
2. In a large mixing bowl, cream the butter until smooth.
3. Beat in the vanilla extract and sugar until well combined.
4. Add the egg and beat until thoroughly mixed.
5. Gradually add the sifted dry ingredients to the wet mixture on low speed, scraping the bowl with a rubber spatula until the mixture holds together.
6. Transfer the dough to a small bowl for easy handling and let it rest at room temperature.
7. Prepare the filling: In a small bowl, mix 3/4 cup smooth peanut butter (not chunky) with 1/2 cup sifted or strained confectioners' sugar until thoroughly combined.
8. Preheat the oven to 325°F (163°C).
9. To shape the cookies:
a. On a large piece of wax paper, place mounds of dough using about 1 rounded tablespoon per mound. Do not make them too large; measure with a measuring spoon.
b. Place mounds of filling on separate pieces of waxed paper using about 1/2 level teaspoon for each mound.
10. Roll one mound of dough into a ball and flatten it between your palms until very thin.
11. Place the filling in the center of the flattened dough, then bring the edges around to seal.
12. Roll the filled dough into a cylindrical shape about 2 inches long with slightly tapered ends.
13. Place the cookie on an unbuttered baking sheet, turning the ends down slightly to form a short, fat crescent.
14. Repeat steps 9-13 for all cookies, spacing them 1/2 to 1 inch apart.
15. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes or until firm to the touch. Turn the cookie sheet front to back once during baking for even cooking.
16. Allow cookies to cool on wire racks before serving.
